title: Fanout Exchanges and True Publish/Subscribe · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective fanout subscription topology and lifecycle map; preserve message identity and timing, locate the first broken ownership boundary, and retain a deterministic regression case. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in fanout subscription topology and lifecycle map and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evide…
objectives: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy.
Use fanout routing when every binding should match and record the resulting copy count and storage responsibility.
Design exclusive server-named subscriptions for session-scoped state and account for disconnect, binding, declaration, and cleanup races.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug messaging team handles case 7367 in a partner publisher using an unroutable changed topic key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove one subscription queue per responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then make the broker confirmation disappear after the message may have committed. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy.

Inspect the Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy. steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Late Subscriber Semantics: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug messaging team handles case 8711 in a partner publisher using an unroutable changed topic key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove one subscription queue per responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Late Subscriber Semantics.
steps.why: Retrieving Late Subscriber Semantics through the concrete fanout subscription topology and lifecycle map case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Late Subscriber Semantics, keep Late Subscriber Semantics connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path.

hints: For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Late Subscriber Semantics, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Late Subscriber Semantics precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Late Subscriber Semantics, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug messaging team handles case 8711 in a partner publisher using an unroutable changed topic key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove one subscription queue per responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Late Subscriber Semantics, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A new fanout binding automatically receives messages published before it existed.
